report_name: EU Commission Data Breach Attributed to TeamPCP Hacking Group via Trivy Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A hacking group that conducted a sophisticated supply chain attack against the European Commission by compromising the Trivy vulnerability scanner. They exfiltrated 92GB of data from an AWS account and collaborated with ShinyHunters to sell it.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltrate and sell sensitive data from government entities for financial profit.

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious data breach broker group that collaborated with TeamPCP by advertising the stolen European Commission data for sale on their dark web forum.
threat_actors.goals: Broker and sell stolen data on dark web forums.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The attackers compromised the distribution mechanism for the Trivy vulnerability scanner to inject malicious code into an update, which was then delivered to the European Commission.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The European Commission unknowingly executed malicious code by installing a trojanized version of the Trivy scanner through its standard software update procedures.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_patterns.description: The malicious code within the compromised Trivy scanner scanned its cloud environment to find and exfiltrate a secret AWS API key.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used the stolen AWS API key to access the European Commission's AWS services and exfiltrate approximately 92GB of data.

malware.name: Trivy Backdoor
malware.description: A malicious backdoor injected into a legitimate version of the Trivy vulnerability scanner. The backdoor was designed to find and exfiltrate AWS API keys from the environment it was executed in.

tools.name: Trivy
tools.description: A popular open-source vulnerability scanner that was compromised in a supply chain attack and used as the vector to breach the European Commission's AWS environment.

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: The primary victim of the data breach. The attackers exfiltrated 92GB of data from its Amazon Web Services (AWS) account.

identities.name: European Union Entities
identities.description: A class of victims whose data, including names and email information from at least 29 different EU entities, was compromised in the breach.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Trivy Distribution Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The software update mechanism for the Trivy scanner that was compromised by TeamPCP to distribute a backdoored version of the tool.

infrastructure.name: Compromised AWS Account
infrastructure.description: The European Commission's Amazon Web Services account that was accessed by attackers using a stolen API key to exfiltrate data.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Forum
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum operated by the ShinyHunters group where the data stolen from the European Commission was advertised for sale.

indicators.name: Unexpected Egress from Trivy Scanner
indicators.pattern:
title: Unexpected Egress from Trivy Scanner
id: 2f8b1c9a-7d3e-4f5a-8b1c-9d0e1f2a3b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects unexpected outbound network connections from the Trivy vulnerability scanner, which could indicate a compromised version exfiltrating data like API keys.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/european-commission-data-breach-linked-to-trivy-supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-07
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate outbound connections made by Trivy for updates or to access vulnerability databases.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/trivy'
    Initiated: true
  filter_expected:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'ghcr.io'
      - 'pkg-containers.github.io'
      - 'registry.k8s.io'
      - 'docker.io'
      - 'registry-1.docker.io'
  condition: selection and not filter_expected
indicators.description: Detects unexpected outbound network connections originating from the Trivy process. A compromised version of Trivy would need to make an external connection to exfiltrate stolen credentials, such as an AWS API key.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
